path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\01. WarioWare, Inc..mp3
description: This main theme from WarioWare is so fucking good. It's such a sleazy vibe and I never get tired of it. This remixes the Title and Menu themes and it's great.
title: WarioWare, Inc.
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\02. WarioWare, Inc. Medley.mp3
description: This medley is genuinely great! It really captures the chaotic vibe of the game perfectly. I'm so impressed that they managed to cram so much of the game into it, including one of my all-time favourite pieces of Nintendo music 'Drifting Away' from the Dribble & Spitz stage. And then topping it off with the title theme? Great.
title: WarioWare, Inc. Medley
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\03. Mona Pizza's Song.mp3
description: I really struggle with Mona Pizza's song. I never played Twisted so I don't associate Mona with pizza delivery. All the different voices that come in... it's not enough to get me interested.
title: Mona Pizza's Song
composers: Kenichi Nishimaki, Masanobu Matsunaga, Yasuhisa Baba
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\04. Mona Pizza's Song (JP).mp3
description: This one is the same as the other one except in Japanese. I also don't like it.
title: Mona Pizza's Song (JP)
composers: Kenichi Nishimaki, Masanobu Matsunaga, Yasuhisa Baba
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\05. Ashley's Song.mp3
description: Now this one is genuinely great. The lyrics are funny and I love the drama of it. It's also kinda jazzy. 'She can rule the world and still finish all her homework', great. The break with the muted trumpet at the end? Great.
title: Ashley's Song
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\06. Ashley's Song (JP) (Brawl).mp3
description: The voice for the japanese version is really breathy and weird and I'm not used to it!!
title: Ashley's Song (JP)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\07. Ashley's Song (JP) (for 3DS & Wii U).mp3
description: They added this bizarre remix in Smash 4 but I think it sounds terrible. Nothing comes close to the original in terms of style and drama.
title: Ashley's Song (JP)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\08. Mike's Song.mp3
description: Bizarre song about karaoke from this character from 'Touched'. He never came back after this. It's a 12-bar blues which I really dislike.
title: Mike's Song
composers: Masanobu Matsunaga, Yasuhisa Baba, Masaru Tajima
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\09. Mike's Song (JP).mp3
description: The Japanese version of this is really confounding. Why did they make it sound like this. Why does this woman put of a cutesy voice like this.
title: Mike's Song (JP)
composers: Masanobu Matsunaga, Yasuhisa Baba, Masaru Tajima
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\10. Ruins - Wario Land - Shake It!.mp3
description: Wario Land: Shake It! has an excellent soundtrack and they did well to put this in but why on earth is this the only song from the Wario Land series?? They even made Wario's victory theme to be this as well which is totally confusing, why would they do that but otherwise completely ignore Wario Land? This track is great though.
title: Ruins
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 16 - WarioWare (3DS, Switch, Wii, Wii U) (gamerip) (2008)\11. Gamer.mp3
description: They put this in for the Game & Wario stage. It should play when the mum spots you, and otherwise it's silent. Silence in smash is just so weird so the idea totally didn't work. And if you play this on any other stage, it's just this horrible terrifying loop. They should remove this from the game because people are out here selecting this song in tournament out of spite.
title: Gamer
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 16: Wario
